title: transfer — trait composition
description: Owns `HasImportable` + `HasExportable` — the opt-in traits models pull in alongside their #[Importable] / #[Exportable] attribute. Also owns the trait composition on our own XferJob + XferShard + XferArtifact + XferMappingProfile.

owned.description: Opt-in trait for models registering with the import pipeline. Wires the paired #[Importable] attribute + #[ImportField] / #[TransferField] class-level attributes into the EntityRegistry at compile time; provides `->importScope()` + `->importSchema()` runtime accessors.
usedBy: any domain module opting a model into imports (athletes, teams, invitations, newsletter subscribers, ...)
notes: Also registers a `resolveTransferFields()` static method the compiler calls to merge attribute discovery with the dynamic `transferSchema()` escape hatch.
Composes with domain traits — BelongsToWorkspace + HasUlids + HasActivityLog + HasAudit as needed by the model.
Does NOT force the model to implement any Laravel Excel concern — the AbstractDynamicImport handles that.

owned.description: Symmetric opt-in trait for the export pipeline. Wires #[Exportable] + #[ExportField] / #[TransferField] class-level attributes into the EntityRegistry; provides `->exportScope()` + `->exportSchema()` accessors.
usedBy: any domain module opting a model into exports
notes: Registers a `resolveTransferFields()` static method (same as HasImportable — the two traits merge cleanly when both are used).

foundation.reason: Free-form platform notes on the operation record.

activity.reason: Every XferJob status transition writes a product-feed row. Mapping-profile CRUD writes too.

audit.reason: Every XferJob status transition writes a compliance-grade audit row. Values redact PII per data-classes.json.

maatwebsite_excel.reason: Collects row-level validation failures for the errors artifact writer.

xfer_job.notes: The full stack. HasActivityLog + HasAudit together write to both the product feed and the compliance trail on every transition.

xfer_shard.notes: Lightweight — no dual-write. Aggregate-level auditing on the parent XferJob covers it.

xfer_mapping_profile.notes: HasActivityLog covers CRUD (create / update / share / delete) so the workspace admin can see 'Alice created mapping profile Q3 HubSpot Import'. No HasAudit — mapping profiles aren't compliance material.
